name: ActiveConnections
definition: Number of concurrent active TCP connections through the PrivateLink endpoint or endpoint service during the 1-minute CloudWatch period. Measures sustained connection load; sudden drops indicate mass disconnections from idle timeout or backend failures.
absence_means: Endpoint/service exists but no active connections established; either no consumer traffic or all connections recently closed.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for typical deployments (10-100 endpoints per account); high cardinality risk if grouping by subnet id (1000s). Use vpc endpoint id (top 10) or service name (top 10) only. Context fields use SPACE-SEPARATED naming.

name: NewConnections
definition: Number of new TCP connections established through the PrivateLink endpoint or endpoint service during the 1-minute CloudWatch period. Measures connection establishment rate; zero NewConnections while app attempts connections = authorization or connectivity failure.
absence_means: No new connections during period; normal if connection pool stable. Zero while app attempts connections = rejected/failed state, security group block, or DNS failure.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for group-by on vpc endpoint id or service name (low-medium cardinality). Cumulative temporality confirmed; use rate post-function.

name: BytesProcessed
definition: Total bytes transferred through the PrivateLink endpoint or endpoint service during the 1-minute CloudWatch period. Includes both inbound and outbound traffic. Used for bandwidth utilization analysis and data transfer cost calculation ($0.01/GB tier 1).
absence_means: No data transferred during period; endpoint/service exists but idle. Zero sustained for >5 minutes = possible connectivity issue or app not using endpoint.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for vpc endpoint id/service name group-by. NOTE: Availability Zone dimension does NOT exist in Tsuga; per-AZ bandwidth analysis not possible. Region dimension (context.cloud.region) exists for cross-region cost analysis.

name: PacketDropCount
definition: Number of packets dropped by the PrivateLink endpoint or endpoint service during the 1-minute CloudWatch period. Primary indicator of bandwidth saturation (exceeding 10 Gbps per AZ), MTU mismatches, or network congestion. Any sustained non-zero value is actionable.
absence_means: No packets dropped; healthy state. Zero is expected baseline; any non-zero indicates issues.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for vpc endpoint id/service name group-by. NOTE: Availability Zone dimension does NOT exist; cannot identify per-AZ hotspots. Aggregate at endpoint level only.

name: ResetPacketsReceived
definition: Number of TCP RST (reset) packets received by the PrivateLink endpoint or endpoint service during the 1-minute CloudWatch period. Indicates abrupt connection terminations from idle timeout (350s), application errors, or NLB target health failures. Elevated RST count (>100/min) = active connection instability.
absence_means: No TCP resets; healthy connection lifecycle (graceful FIN terminations). Some RST packets normal during failover or app restarts; sustained >100/min is actionable.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for vpc endpoint id/service name group-by. Correlate with NLB UnHealthyHostCount (provider-side) to identify backend-driven resets vs idle timeout resets. Cumulative temporality confirmed; use rate post-function.

name: EndpointsCount
definition: (Provider-side OR aggregate metric) Count of VPC endpoints. Exact semantics unclear; may be total consumer endpoints per service or aggregate endpoint count. Unexpected metric found during discovery.
absence_means: Zero endpoints; no consumers connected to endpoint service OR metric not applicable to consumer-side.
cardinality_footguns: Low cardinality; safe for service name group-by. Exact use case unclear; Stage 3 should evaluate if worth including in provider dashboards.

name: NLB_HealthyHostCount
definition: (Provider-side only) Number of healthy targets registered with the Network Load Balancer backing the PrivateLink endpoint service. Target health failures cascade to consumer-side metrics (PacketDropCount, ResetPacketsReceived). Zero healthy hosts = service unavailable to all consumers.
absence_means: NLB has no registered targets OR all targets are failing health checks. Zero sustained >2 minutes = service outage.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for service id and target group arn group-by (low cardinality in typical provider setups). Provider-side only; consumers cannot see this metric. Context field naming uses space-separated format (inferred based on PrivateLink pattern; Stage 3 should validate).

name: NLB_UnHealthyHostCount
definition: (Provider-side only) Number of unhealthy targets registered with the Network Load Balancer backing the PrivateLink endpoint service. Unhealthy targets cause consumer-side connection errors and resets. Any non-zero value is actionable for provider.
absence_means: All targets are healthy. Zero is expected baseline; any non-zero indicates backend service degradation.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for service id and target group arn group-by. Note: Tsuga metric name uses underscore in un_healthy (not unhealthy). Correlate with consumer-side ResetPacketsReceived to confirm backend-driven connection failures.

name: NLB_TargetConnectionErrorCount
definition: (Provider-side only) Number of connections from the NLB to targets that failed during the 1-minute period. Indicates backend capacity exhaustion, target crashes, or port mismatches. Correlates with consumer-side RST resets.
absence_means: No connection errors to backend targets; healthy state. Zero is expected; any non-zero indicates backend issues.
cardinality_footguns: NOT FOUND in Tsuga during Stage 2 discovery. Metric expected but not ingested. Alternative: use aws_networkelb_tcp_target_reset_count as proxy for connection quality. Widget using this metric should be gated or replaced.

name: NLB_ProcessedBytes
definition: (Provider-side only) Total bytes processed by the Network Load Balancer backing the PrivateLink endpoint service. Should closely match consumer-side BytesProcessed; significant divergence indicates traffic loss or asymmetric routing.
absence_means: NLB processed no traffic; endpoint service exists but no consumer connections. Zero sustained = no consumer usage.
cardinality_footguns: Safe for service id and target group arn group-by. Cumulative temporality confirmed; use rate post-function.

name: metric family: Hourly Endpoint Charge
definition: Fixed $0.01/hour cost per interface VPC endpoint (consumer-side). Not a CloudWatch metric; calculated from endpoint count * hours. Resource endpoints cost $0.02/hour. Total hourly cost = (interface endpoint count * $0.01) + (resource endpoint count * $0.02).
absence_means: Endpoint does not exist or is terminated. Zero cost means no active endpoints.
cardinality_footguns: Safe; endpoint count per account typically <1000. Calculation requires endpoint count metric (not a standard CloudWatch metric; derive from DescribeVpcEndpoints API or tag-based tracking).
variants: No direct CloudWatch metric exists. Cost calculated from endpoint count (ListMetrics or DescribeVpcEndpoints API) * $0.01/hour. Not included in dashboard scope (requires external API call).

name: metric family: Data Processing Charge
definition: Variable cost per GB of data processed through PrivateLink endpoints. Tiered pricing: $0.01/GB (first PB), $0.006/GB (next 4 PB), $0.004/GB (5+ PB). Calculated from BytesProcessed CloudWatch metric. Formula: BytesProcessed (GB) * tier rate. Does NOT include cross-region data transfer ($0.02/GB additional).
widget_suggestions: query-value (derived from BytesProcessed, not standalone metric)
absence_means: No data processed; endpoint exists but idle. Zero cost for data processing (hourly charge still applies).
cardinality_footguns: Safe; same cardinality as BytesProcessed. Cross-region data transfer requires context.cloud.region dimension to apply $0.02/GB surcharge.
variants: Derived from aws_privatelinkendpoints_bytes_processed CloudWatch metric. Tiered pricing requires step function: if bytes < 1 PB: rate $0.01/GB; elif bytes < 5 PB: rate $0.006/GB; else: rate $0.004/GB.

name: metric family: Cross-Region Data Transfer Charge
definition: Additional $0.02/GB charge for data transferred across AWS regions via cross-region PrivateLink (on top of data processing charge). Only applies when consumer and provider are in different regions. Calculated from BytesProcessed with Region dimension filter. Formula: BytesProcessed (GB, cross-region only) * $0.02.
widget_suggestions: query-value (derived from BytesProcessed filtered by Region, not standalone metric)
absence_means: No cross-region data transferred; either no cross-region endpoints deployed or all traffic is intra-region. Zero cost for cross-region transfer (intra-region data processing charge still applies).
cardinality_footguns: Requires context.cloud.region dimension to identify cross-region vs intra-region traffic. High cardinality if many regions; typically 1-5 active regions. Cross-region filtering logic needs validation in Stage 3.
variants: Derived from aws_privatelinkendpoints_bytes_processed CloudWatch metric filtered where consumer region != provider region. Requires cross-region connectivity enabled on endpoint service.
